title: digitaria ischaemum var. hirta
intro: Digitaria ischaemum var. hirta, also known as Dallis grass or Hairy finger grass, is a type of grassy weed that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Digitaria ischaemum var. hirta include its use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of this plant involve infusions and decoctions, where the leaves and stems are typically used in traditional medicine to create remedies for various health conditions.
